name: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools
about: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools serves approximately 139,000 students in Charlotte and Mecklenburg County, North Carolina, and is the second largest school district in the state.
calendarNotes: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Schools follows the standard North Carolina academic calendar, starting in late August and including a Fall Break in October. Spring Break typically falls in mid-to-late March. CMS has a large number of magnet programs — from STEM academies to arts-focused schools — that follow the standard calendar but have separate enrollment deadlines in January for the following school year. Charlotte's rapid growth as a financial center and tech hub means enrollment patterns shift frequently. Families coordinating with relatives in neighboring South Carolina counties should be aware that SC school calendars run on a different schedule with different Spring Break timing, particularly for districts in the greater Charlotte metro.
